volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 3.3.440 - Section 4.3.516)

sections.label: Section 3.3.440
sections.original: non hercle , quoi nunc hoc dem spectandum , scio .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I don ’ t know to whom now to give this money to be tested [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.441
sections.translation: Perhaps you are afraid to entrust it to any one ’ s hands ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.442
sections.original: Mirum quin ⋯ citius iam a foro argentarii
sections.translation: Strange if I wasn ’ t . More quickly , now-a-days , do bankers abscond from the Forum , than a wheel spins round in a race .

sections.label: Section 3.3.444
sections.original: Abi istác travorsis angiportis ad forum ;
sections.translation: Do you go that way , through the alleys , the back way to the Forum [*] ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.445
sections.original: eadem ístaec facito mulier ad me transeat
sections.translation: let this damsel pass through the same way to our house , through the garden .

sections.label: Section 3.3.446b
sections.translation: I ’ ll have her here this moment .

sections.label: Section 3.3.446c
sections.translation: But not in public view .

sections.label: Section 3.3.447
sections.translation: Very discreet .

sections.label: Section 3.3.447b
sections.translation: To-morrow she must go to return thanks [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.448
sections.translation: I ’ faith , just so indeed .

sections.label: Section 3.3.448b
sections.translation: While you ’ ve been loitering , you might have got back .

sections.label: Section 4.1.449
sections.original: si quam rem accures sobrie aut frugaliter ,
sections.translation: If you give attention to any matter with steadiness or with good management ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.450
sections.original: solet illa recte sub manus succedere .
sections.translation: that same is wont properly to thrive to your satisfaction . And , by my faith , pretty nearly according as each man gives attention to his business , in the same manner do the results [*] finally ensure him success . If he is knavish or a rogue , the business turns out badly which he has commenced ; but if he uses good management , it results profitably .

sections.label: Section 4.1.455
sections.original: hanc ego rem exorsus sum facete et callide ,
sections.translation: Cleverly and skilfully did I commence upon this business ; for that reason do I trust that it will turn out well for me . Now , I ’ ll this day have the Procurer so hampered , that he shan ’ t know himself which way to extricate himself . ( Goes to the door .) Sagaristio , hallo ! Come forth , and bring out the young woman ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.460
sections.original: et istás tabellas , quas consignavi tibi ,
sections.translation: and that letter which I sealed for you , which you brought me all the way from Persia , from my master .

sections.label: Section 4.2.462
sections.translation: Have I delayed at all ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.462b
sections.translation: Bravo ! bravo ! dressed out in splendid style . ( To SAGARISTIO .) The tiara [*] does finely set off your dress . Then , too , how beautifully does the slipper become this stranger damsel !

sections.label: Section 4.2.465
sections.translation: But are you thoroughly up in your parts ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.465b
sections.translation: Tragedians and Comedians have never been up so well .

sections.label: Section 4.2.466b
sections.translation: Troth , you are giving me kind assistance . Come , be off that way ( pointing .) to a distance out of sight , and hold your tongue . When you see me conversing with the Procurer , that will be the time to accost us ; now be off , you ,— away with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.470
sections.original: Quoí homini di própitii sunt , áliquid obiciúnt lucri ;
sections.translation: The man to whom the Deities are propitious , in his way they throw some profit . For I this day have made a saving of two loaves daily ; this way , she who this day was my slave is now her own ; by his cash he has prevailed ; this day then she ’ ll be dining at the expense of another , she ’ ll be tasting nothing of mine . Am I not a worthy man , am I not a courteous citizen , who this day have made the extensive state of Attica

sections.label: Section 4.3.475
sections.original: maximam maiorem feci atque auxi civi femina ?
sections.translation: still larger , and increased it by a female citizen ? But how obliging have I been to-day ! To how many have I given credit , and have from no person taken surety ; so readily did I give credit to all : and I don ’ t fear that of those whom I ’ ve trusted to-day any one will forswear himself against me upon trial . I wish from this day forth to be honest — a thing that never will be and never was .

sections.label: Section 4.3.480
sections.original: Hunc hominem ego hodie in trasennam doctis deducam dolis ,
sections.translation: This fellow , this very day , by clever contrivances , I ’ ll catch in a springe ; and so the snare is cunningly laid for him ; I ’ ll accost the fellow . ( Aloud .) What are you about ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.482b
sections.translation: Giving credit .

sections.label: Section 4.3.482c
sections.translation: Whence do you betake yourself , Dordalus ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.482d
sections.translation: I ’ m going to give you credit [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.3.483
sections.translation: May the Gods grant whatever you may desire .

sections.label: Section 4.3.483b
sections.original: Eho , an iám manu emisisti mulierem ?
sections.translation: How now , have you given the damsel her liberty by this ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.484
sections.translation: I ’ m going to give you credit , i ’ faith , I ’ m going to give you credit , I repeat .

sections.label: Section 4.3.484b
sections.translation: Are you now increased in number by one freed-woman ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.484c
sections.translation: You worry me to death .

sections.label: Section 4.3.485_486
sections.translation: Why , I tell you that I ’ ll give you credit .

sections.label: Section 4.3.485_486b
sections.translation: Tell me in sober truth , is she now at liberty ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.485_486c
sections.translation: Go , go to the Forum , to the Praetor [*] ; make all enquiries , since you don ’ t choose to give me credit . She is at liberty , I say . Do you hear me at all ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.488b
sections.original: At tibi di bene faciant omnes .
sections.translation: May all the Deities bless you then . And never from this time forward , will I wish to you or yours what you don ’ t wish .

sections.label: Section 4.3.490
sections.translation: Be off : don ’ t be swearing that . I quite believe you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.491
sections.translation: Where is your freed-woman now ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.491b
sections.translation: At your house .

sections.label: Section 4.3.491c
sections.translation: Do you say so ? Is she at our house ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.491d
sections.original: Aio , inquam , apud te ést , inquam .
sections.translation: I do say so , I tell you ; she is at your house , I say .

sections.label: Section 4.3.492
sections.original: Ita mé di ament , ut ob ístam rem tibi múlta bona instant á me .
sections.translation: So may the Deities favour me , for this thing many blessings from me are in store for you : for there ’ s a certain matter , which I refrained from mentioning to you ; now I ’ ll disclose it , and from it you can make a very large profit . I ’ ll give you cause to remember me so long as you exist .

sections.label: Section 4.3.495b
sections.original: Bene dictis tuís bene facta aurés meae auxilium expóscunt .
sections.translation: My ears are wanting some kind deeds by way of assistance to these kind words .

sections.label: Section 4.3.496
sections.original: Tuom prómeritumst , merito út faciam . et ut mé scias esse ita fácturum ,
sections.translation: It ’ s only your deserts , that I should do as you deserve . And that you may know that I will do so , take this letter ( showing him a letter .) read it over .

sections.label: Section 4.3.497b
sections.translation: What has this got to do with me ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.497c
sections.original: Immo ád te attinént et tuá refert .
sections.translation: Why yes , it bears reference to yourself , and it does relate to you . But it has just now been brought me from Persia , from my master .

sections.label: Section 4.3.498b
sections.translation: When ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.498c
sections.translation: Not long since .

sections.label: Section 4.3.499
sections.translation: What does it say ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.499b
sections.original: Percóntare ex ipsís . ipsae tibi nárrabunt .
sections.translation: Make enquiry of its own self : it will tell you itself .

sections.label: Section 4.3.500
sections.translation: Give it me , then .

sections.label: Section 4.3.500b
sections.translation: But read it aloud .

sections.label: Section 4.3.500c
sections.translation: Be silent while I read it over .

sections.label: Section 4.3.500d
sections.translation: I ’ ll not utter a word .

sections.label: Section 4.3.501
sections.translation: Timarchides sends health to Toxilus and all the family . If you are well , I am glad ; I am quite well , and carrying on my business , and am making money ; and I am not able to return home for these eight months ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.505
sections.original: itaque hic est quod me detinet negotium .
sections.translation: for there is some business which detains me here ; the Persians have taken Chrysopolis [*] , a city of Arabia , full of good things , and an ancient town ; there the booty is being collected , that a public auction may be made ; this matter causes me to be absent from home .

sections.label: Section 4.3.510
sections.original: operam atque hospitium ego isti praehiberi volo ,
sections.translation: I wish attention and hospitality to be shown to the person who brings this letter to you . Attend to what he wants ; for at his own house at home he has shown me the greatest attentions .

sections.label: Section 4.3.513
sections.original: Quíd id ad me aut ad meam rem refert , Persae quid rerum gerant
sections.translation: What has it to do with me or my welfare , what matters the Persians are about , or what your master is doing ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.514b
sections.original: Tace , stultiloque ; nescis quid te instet boni
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , silly babbler ; you don ’ t know what blessing awaits you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.515
sections.original: neque quam tibi Fortuna faculam lucrifera adlucere volt .
sections.translation: It ’ s in vain that Fortune is ready to light for you her torch that leads to profit .

sections.label: Section 4.3.516
sections.translation: What Fortune is this that leads to profit ?
